title: Rename Catalog to Inventory, Add Buy/Sell Price Fields, and Goods/Service Type Selector

requirements.summary: Rename all occurrences of 'Catalog' / 'Katalog' to 'Inventory' across the frontend — including the sidebar navigation label, page title, route path, and any internal references.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar link reads 'Inventory' instead of 'Katalog' or 'Catalog'
The route path is updated to '/inventory'
Page heading on the page itself reads 'Inventory'
No remaining references to 'Katalog' or 'Catalog' appear in the UI

file_operations.description: Update the navigation link label from 'Katalog' to 'Inventory' and change the route path from '/catalog' to '/inventory'. Ensure the icon (Package) remains the same and the active state highlighting works correctly with the new path.
Update the route path from '/catalog' to '/inventory' in the TanStack Router configuration. Change the import statement to reference the renamed page component (InventoryPage instead of CatalogPage).

Rename the file from CatalogPage.tsx to InventoryPage.tsx. Update the component name from CatalogPage to InventoryPage. Change the page heading from 'Catalog' or 'Katalog' to 'Inventory'. Update all internal references, comments, and string literals that mention 'catalog' or 'katalog' to use 'inventory' instead.

requirements.summary: Add 'selling price' (harga jual) and 'purchase price' (harga beli) fields to the frontend UI — update all add/edit dialogs in the Inventory page to include these two fields and display both prices in the item list table.
acceptanceCriteria: Add and edit dialogs in Inventory page show labeled input fields for 'Selling Price' and 'Purchase Price'
Both prices are displayed in the inventory item list
Existing items without the new fields do not cause runtime errors (default to 0 or 0.0)

file_operations.description: Add 'Selling Price' and 'Purchase Price' input fields (type number, required) to both the add and edit product/service dialogs. Update the form state to include sellingPrice and purchasePrice fields. Add two new columns to the inventory list table to display both prices formatted as Indonesian Rupiah using the formatRupiah utility. Update the mutation calls (addInventoryItem) to pass sellingPrice and purchasePrice from the form. Ensure default values of 0 are used when existing items lack these fields to prevent runtime errors.

requirements.summary: Add an item type selector ('Goods' / 'Service') to the frontend — update the Inventory add/edit dialogs to include a type selector that conditionally shows or hides the qty field based on the selected type.
acceptanceCriteria: Add/edit dialog contains a type selector (e.g., toggle or dropdown) labeled 'Goods' / 'Service'
When 'Service' is selected, the qty/stock input field is hidden in the dialog
Service items display 'Unlimited' or '∞' in the stock/qty column of the inventory list
Goods items continue to show and require a numeric qty/stock value

file_operations.description: Add a type selector (RadioGroup or Select component from shadcn/ui) to the add and edit dialogs with options 'Goods' and 'Service', defaulting to 'Goods'. Add productType to the form state (using the ProductType enum from backend.d.ts with values 'goods' or 'service'). Conditionally render the quantity/stock input field only when productType is 'goods'; hide it when 'service' is selected. In the inventory list table, display 'Unlimited' or '∞' in the stock/qty column for service items, and show the numeric quantity for goods items. Update mutation calls to include the productType field. Ensure the backend ProductType enum values match the frontend selection.

requirements.summary: Update the Profit/Loss section in the Reports page to calculate and display profit using the selling price minus purchase price per item sold, aggregated across transactions in the selected period.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports page shows gross profit calculated as sum of (sellingPrice - purchasePrice) × quantity for all sold goods items
Profit/Loss figures are displayed in Indonesian Rupiah format consistent with the rest of the app
The calculation covers the same date filters used by existing daily/monthly report tabs

file_operations.description: Add a new React Query hook (e.g., useProfitLoss) that calls the backend calculateProfitLoss method with startTime and endTime parameters (converted to nanosecond bigints using the dateToNanos utility). The hook should accept date range parameters and return the profit/loss value as a bigint. Follow the same pattern as existing query hooks in this file.

Add a new 'Profit/Loss' tab to the existing tabs (Daily, Monthly, Top Selling). In this tab, provide date range inputs (start date and end date) similar to the daily report tab. Call the useProfitLoss hook with the selected date range. Display the returned profit/loss value formatted as Indonesian Rupiah using the formatRupiah utility. Show appropriate loading and error states. Include a brief description that the profit is calculated as (selling price - purchase price) × quantity for all goods items sold in the period.
